summary: Iteration 15 — Complete end-to-end business logic testing of 7 flows with math verification, DB persistence, and state transitions. Fixed 2 critical bugs discovered during testing. 40/42 tests passed (2 skipped for Flow 7 architectural mismatch).

bugs_fixed_during_testing.bug: Missing 'timezone' import — `from datetime import datetime, timedelta` was missing `timezone`
bugs_fixed_during_testing.impact: POST /api/dipendenti/contratti returned HTTP 500 with NameError on EVERY call
bugs_fixed_during_testing.fix: Changed to `from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone`

bugs_fixed_during_testing.bug: POST /api/cedolini/stima only checked `employees` collection for dipendente validation, not `dipendenti`
bugs_fixed_during_testing.impact: Any dipendente from GET /api/dipendenti would fail stima with 404 (different collection)
bugs_fixed_during_testing.fix: Added fallback: if not found in 'employees', check 'dipendenti' collection
bugs_fixed_during_testing.note: The fix for /dipendente/{id} was already applied in previous iteration but stima had the same bug

FLOW1_food_cost.details: costo_totale=(0.5*1.0)+(0.2*8.0)=€2.10 ✓, costo_porzione=2.10/4=€0.525 ✓, riepilogo shows correct values, DB persistence verified, cleanup done

FLOW2_lotti.details: Create lotto with quantita=50 kg, stato='attivo' verified in list and single GET, DELETE+404 confirmed

FLOW3_contratti.details: Fixed timezone NameError. GET /contratti returns 200 (route ordering fix confirmed). Create/list/update(1800→1900)/termina/verify-terminato all pass. Contratto termina sets stato='terminato' (note: review_request says 'concluso' but code uses 'terminato')

FLOW4_cedolini.details: Fixed stima collection fallback. Math check lordo≈netto+ritenute with tolerance €1 PASSED. GET /cedolini/dipendente/{id} returns 200 (collection fallback working)

FLOW5_prima_nota_cassa.details: uscita €100 created, balance updated (saldo decreased by €100 ✓), cleanup done. NOTE: default list (limit=100 sorted by date desc) may hide older movements — test uses limit=1000

FLOW6_ordini_fornitori.details: Ordine created as bozza, appears in /bozze list, counter incremented, required fields present. NOTE: GET /api/cucina/ordini-fornitori root returns 405 (no list endpoint, by design)

FLOW7_riconciliazione_bonifici.reason: Architectural mismatch: POST /api/archivio-bonifici/associa-fattura uses MongoDB ObjectId on 'archivio_bonifici' collection, but GET /api/archivio-bonifici/transfers reads from 'bonifici_transfers' collection. Transfer IDs are string UUIDs, not compatible with ObjectId. Association fails with 400 'ID non valido'.

critical.issue: Collection mismatch: associa-fattura operates on 'archivio_bonifici' collection using MongoDB ObjectId, but GET /transfers reads from 'bonifici_transfers' collection with custom string IDs. Cannot associate transfers obtained from /transfers with fatture because the bonifico_id would need to be an ObjectId from archivio_bonifici.
critical.rca: Two separate collections: 'bonifici_transfers' (PDF-imported transfers with string IDs) and 'archivio_bonifici' (older/manual entries with ObjectId). The UI flow that links transfers→invoices uses the wrong ID type. Fix: either (a) return MongoDB _id as string from /transfers endpoint, or (b) update associa-fattura to also accept transfer_id from bonifici_transfers collection.

minor.issue: Sets stato='terminato' but review_request/documentation says 'concluso'. Minor naming inconsistency.

Default limit=100 (sorted by date desc) can hide newly created movements if DB has many records from past dates. The movement IS saved (saldo updates correctly) but doesn't appear in first page. Use limit=1000+ to reliably find specific movements.

action_items: FIX HIGH (Flow 7): archivio_bonifici associa-fattura uses ObjectId on wrong collection — transfers from /api/archivio-bonifici/transfers have UUID string IDs, but associa-fattura expects MongoDB ObjectId from 'archivio_bonifici' collection. Need to reconcile collections or update endpoint to accept transfer_id from 'bonifici_transfers' collection.
MINOR: POST /contratti/{id}/termina sets stato='terminato' but docs say 'concluso' — standardize naming.

MINOR: GET /api/prima-nota/cassa only returns 100 items by default (sorted by date desc) — newer movements with older data dates may not appear in first page. Consider adding a filter by specific ID or higher default limit.
critical_code_review_comments: dipendenti.py was missing `timezone` from `from datetime import datetime, timedelta` — causing every call to create_contratto to fail with 500. This is a Python import bug at the module level that should have been caught by startup.

cedolini.py /stima endpoint: while /dipendente/{id} had the fallback fix applied (check both 'employees' and 'dipendenti'), the /stima endpoint was NOT fixed in the previous iteration — same NameError pattern fixed now.

Flow 7 architectural issue: bonifici_transfers (PDF-imported) vs archivio_bonifici (older system) are two separate collections. The reconciliation UI flow links these but the API IDs are incompatible. This is a data architecture issue requiring either collection unification or endpoint adaptation.
updated_files: /app/app/routers/cedolini.py (added fallback to dipendenti collection in stima endpoint)

success_rate.backend: 95% (40/42 tests pass, 2 skipped for Flow 7 architectural mismatch, 0 failures after fixes)

seed_data_creation: None — all test data created and cleaned up during test run
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 7 flows tested end-to-end. Fixed: dipendenti.py timezone import (500→200 for POST contratti), cedolini.py stima fallback (404→200 for dipendenti collection). Remaining issue: Flow 7 riconciliazione bonifici has collection mismatch (archivio_bonifici ObjectId vs bonifici_transfers UUID). All other flows (food cost math, lotti CRUD, contratti full lifecycle, cedolini math, prima nota cassa balance) working correctly.

contratti_500.root_cause: dipendenti.py line 7: `from datetime import datetime, timedelta` — missing `timezone`. The create_contratto function at line 1479 uses `datetime.now(timezone.utc)` but `timezone` was not imported. Python NameError at runtime.
contratti_500.fix_applied: Added timezone to import: `from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone`

cedolini_stima_404.root_cause: calcola_stima_cedolino at line 358 only checked `db['employees']` collection for dipendente. But Collections.EMPLOYEES = 'dipendenti', so the main HR collection is 'dipendenti' not 'employees'. Any ID from GET /api/dipendenti would fail.
cedolini_stima_404.fix_applied: Added fallback: if not found in 'employees', check 'dipendenti' collection (same pattern as /dipendente/{id} endpoint)

bonifici_collection_mismatch.root_cause: GET /api/archivio-bonifici/transfers reads from 'bonifici_transfers' collection (documents with string UUIDs as id). POST /api/archivio-bonifici/associa-fattura uses ObjectId(bonifico_id) on 'archivio_bonifici' collection. These are completely different collections. The UI flow that shows transfers and allows associating them to invoices is broken because the ID types and collections don't match.
bonifici_collection_mismatch.fix_needed: Either: (a) Make /transfers also return documents from 'archivio_bonifici' with their ObjectId as string, OR (b) Update associa-fattura to work with bonifici_transfers collection using string id instead of ObjectId
